paragraphs.body: A Place To Belong, Line by Line It’s the warmth of friendly voices, the quiet moments of comfort, and the memories full of happiness. Home is the place where I find peace. Sometimes, for me, that is the room where I’ve spent most of my days. The room filled with morning light and the smell of my mother’s cooking. It is the laughter from the living room when my dad and I are cracking jokes. As we all know, life is not only about smiles, so this place sometimes it can be the one that most of my arguments between me and the mom started and end it. But I believe that that’s why is so special, because it doesn’t need to be perfect to be my favourite place on the planet.

annotations.comment: This is such a lovely opening paragraph! The tone is so warm and peaceful.
annotations.context_left: , for me, that is the room where

paragraphs.body: Sometimes, home is not just a room, an apartment or a house. It can be a song, a photo, a recipe, or even a journal. Growing up, I learned that starting a new chapter in your life comes with compromises. And like this, you understand that home can travel with you. In school most of the time, teachers tell us that home is a building, constructed by four walls, one roof and two windows. At that period of time, I was thinking that a house is unique because of the area that it is located or how it was built. But not, at twenty years old, my home is not limited to permanence or property. Home isn’t just a built, it is a feeling. And, after you truly experience this moment, you learn to look for it in places beyond brick and wood.

annotations.text: Sometimes, home is not just a room, an apartment or a house. It can be a song, a photo, a recipe, or even a journal.

paragraphs.body: As usual, my journal is in my bag, hidden among my other one hundred random things. But even I change my bags, my journal is all the time next to me, waiting to be scrawled with my thoughts. The cover is a beautiful navy blue that got my eye from day one. The gold spiral embraces all the pages creating in this way, day by day, story of my daily life. Most of the time, when I reach into my bag, my fingers brush against it, and it feels like a small moment of peace in the middle of all the chaos. Sometimes, I catch a subtle scent of ink from the pages, a reminder of when I accidentally spilled it, and that small aspect enhances the journal’s feeling of being truly mine. This item is more than just some thick pages. Is my safe place. Is a place where I can take a moment to pause and reflect, even if only for a minute. Doesn’t matter how hectic the day becomes, the jpurnal provides me with a peaceful momenta space to dream and allow my thoughts to wander freely. That is odd to it is that have so many colours in it and at the same time so many memories. If I look back sometimes is funny to read how I was thinking about some situations and how now, that I got through them, I can see them clearer. Because I got it exactly on the first day when I lead in America, I literally the new chapter of my life wrote on paper, which is a testament or proof of my unique journey and growth.

annotations.text: literally the new chapter of my life wrote on paper, which is a testament or proof of my unique journey and growth.

annotations.context_left: ye from day one. The gold spiral

paragraphs.body: But the first day when I started to write my thoughts, was another moment of a new chapter. It was a day when the alarm was a loud one. The light made its way thought the big blinds, brightening the whole room. I knew it was Sunday, by how the smell of fresh coffee filled the air and surrounded me. My dad was calling me to come into the kitchen because the breakfast was ready. I swung my legs to the side of my bed, uncomfortable because of the coldness that is surrounding me. I stood up for some seconds, lifted my arms over my head, and stretched for a period of time. I went slow, with no rush, to the bathroom. There, the fresh water helped me to wake up properly. After my routine, I followed the smell the coffee and enter in the living room, where I found my parents, watching the news on TV. I was excited by the fact that my dad had made my favourite pancakes. The warmth of the mug in my hands made me think about the present moment and how good it is sometime to slow it down and to have a morning with no rush, no noise, just a peace of mind. Looking on the window, the birds were singing, flying between the young trees from my garden. Simple moments like this made me believe that life is more than just school and volleyball. Sometimes it is about to take it slow and to enjoy even the little things that, most of the time, I don’t even notice them because of how rushed I was.

annotations.text: Looking on the window, the birds were singing, flying between the young trees from my garden. Simple moments like this made me believe that life is more than just school and volleyball.

annotations.comment: I'm interested in the way this paragraph repeats the ideas, and tone, of the intro paragraph. Great move!
annotations.context_left: e of the coldness that is surrounding me. I stood up for some seconds, lifted my arms over my head,

paragraphs.body: And that was the moment that I was thinking that I need to write my thoughts on a piece of paper. It took place there, in my very first room, the one that I still remember the scent of fresh notebook, but at the same time the one of unfamiliarity. That day, when I decided to take a picture of the present moment and remember it for forever. It felt like descovering a piece of home I could take along with me. Every page provided me with a room to relax, to feel the smell of coffee that surrounded me and the sweet taste of my dad’s pancakes, like the first day.

The journal in that moment started to be a home to me. A home that I have it everywhere, even if I am in America or Romania. Through each chaotic doodle or thoughtful penned idea, is the feeling of safety, comfort. The feelings like when I was hearing the voices of my parents or their laughs. For me, this are one of the most beautiful sounds in the world. It makes me feel more me when I am with them. A feeling that nothing can replace it.

annotations.text: A home that I have it everywhere, even if I am in America or Romania. Through each chaotic doodle or thoughtful penned idea, is the feeling of safety, comfort.
annotations.comment: nice :) Lovely details. I wonder if you could "echo" your intro paragraph a bit more intentionally to create a more clear sense of unity for the essay as a whole?
annotations.context_left: to be a home to me.

paragraphs.body: My journal, as you can see, had served as a silent friend during my crazy changes. He took a picture to all my thoughts when I found it hard to express them to the people around me. He provided a spot when my life felt overwhelming. He includes, from my best days in my life to the worst one, to schedules, to vents after practices to calm Sundays. I really can say that is a part of my identity, that is more than what my ID can say. It is more then, where I was born or when. It is my story. A story of a twenty years of living, in this world, full of crazy events that I never saw coming. And for sure this is a reason to be grateful my whole life. But at the time, I feel like all of us need to feel this feeling of being home, but not home. So, where do you go when you need to feel at home?
